name: ION-15098 Network OV Date Deserialization Fix
description: Fix 400 error when changing OV status from UI. OptionalValidation date fields (createdDateUtc, lastModifiedDateUtc) serialized with nanosecond precision by OffsetDateTimeTypeConverter but deserialization expects yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ format.

entries.summary: Fixed Audit.java: removed @JsonFormat causing strict millisecond-only deserialization. Added 11 unit tests.
entries.detail:
Root Cause:
- Audit.java had @JsonFormat(pattern = "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ") on both date getters
- This pattern enforces exactly 3 fractional digits (milliseconds) during deserialization
- But OffsetDateTimeSerializer (from cloud-sdk-aws) outputs ISO_DATE_TIME format with nanosecond precision (e.g., "2025-12-11T13:46:27.348382207Z")
- When UI echoes the full payload back (PUT request), Jackson can't parse the 9-digit fractional seconds → 400 "Unable to process JSON"

Fix:
- Removed @JsonFormat from both getCreatedDateUtc() and getLastModifiedDateUtc() (3 lines deleted)
- Removed unused import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonFormat
- Serialization still uses @JsonSerialize(using = OffsetDateTimeSerializer.class) → ISO format
- Deserialization now handled by JavaTimeModule (from jackson-datatype-jsr310) which accepts any ISO precision
- This matches the pattern used in the auth module's Audit.java (already working in prod)

Tests: 11 new tests in AuditDateSerializationTest, all passing. 117 existing tests still passing.

entries.summary: Committed and pushed fix to remote. 128/128 tests pass.
entries.detail: Committed aba3a10975 on branch bugfix/ION-15098-ov-date-deserialization and pushed to origin. PR creation link: https://git.dev.e2open.com/projects/INT/repos/mercury-services/pull-requests?create&sourceBranch=refs%2Fheads%2Fbugfix%2FION-15098-ov-date-deserialization
